secondGomaaAthanTitle: Second adhan before or after the original one
ThedeviceindicatesthedirectionoftheQibla: The device indicates the direction of the Qibla
Dynamic_display_sub: It allows users to have full control over font size, zoom in and zoom out according to their personal needs. The text can be adjusted to a size suitable for the eyes, which makes reading more comfortable.
Highlight_word_sub:
Enhances understanding of the Qur’an word by word while reading.
The application will be fully implemented when the recitation is started again
coloring_sub: Coloring the signs of verses and the field of the surah
explainAdaptiveModeText: Allows users to fully control the font size and zoom according to their personal needs. The text can be adjusted to a comfortable size for the eyes, making reading more comfortable.
shafiDescription: Asr prayer is calculated in the Shafi'i school when the shadow of an object is equal to its length plus the length of its shadow at noon.
hanafiDescription: Asr prayer is calculated in the Hanafi school when the shadow of an object is twice its length plus the length of its shadow at noon.
calculationMuslimWorldLeagueDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 18° and an Isha angle of 17°
calculationEgyptianDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 19.5° and an Isha angle of 17.5°
calculationKarachiDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 18° and an Isha angle of 18°
calculationUmmAlQuraDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 18.5° and an Isha angle of 90°. Note: A custom adjustment of +30 minutes for Isha during Ramadan should be added.
calculationDubaiDescription: Uses a Fajr and Isha angle of 18.2°
calculationMoonSightingCommitteeDescript: Uses a Fajr angle of 18° and an Isha angle of 18°. Also uses seasonal adjustment values.
calculationNorthAmericaDescription: Included for completeness, but not recommended. Uses a Fajr angle of 15° and an Isha angle of 15°
calculationKuwaitDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 18° and an Isha angle of 17.5°
calculationQatarDescription: A modified version of Umm al-Qura uses a Fajr angle of 18°
calculationSingaporeDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 20° and an Isha angle of 18°
calculationTurkeyDescription: Uses a Fajr angle of 18° and an Isha angle of 17°
calculationTehranTitle: Institute of Geophysics, University of Tehran
calculationTehranDescription: Early Isha time at a 14° angle. Fajr time slightly delayed at a 17.7° angle. Maghrib is calculated based on the sun reaching an angle of 4.5° below the horizon.
fromWherKhatma: From where in the Quran would you like to begin your recitation
nowPrayerTime: It is now time for Adhan
showAskUserForAlarmPermissionDescription: To remind you of prayer times and Azkar, the app needs alarm permission
showAskUserForNotificationsPermissionDes: To remind you of prayer times and Azkar, the app needs notification permission
showQiblaCompassCalibrationDialogDescrip: Calibrate the phone and compass using the following steps:
Move the phone horizontally (left and right)
Move the phone vertically (up and down)
Rotate the phone around itself clockwise and counterclockwise
Warning: Stay away from magnetic objects
showAzkarNotDoneDialogDescription: You have not finished reading Azkar yet, do you want to exit?
showLocationDisabledDialogDescription: Please enable location services to use Qibla.
showNoInternetDialogDescription: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
showAskUserForDownloadTimingDataDescript: Timing files need to be downloaded first for word-by-word. Do you want to download now?
showDownloadFailedDialogDescription: Failed to download the file. Please try again later.
showResetTasbihCountersDialogDescription: Are you sure you want to reset the counters?
showDeleteItemDialogDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
showZkrProgressFoundForContinueDescripti:
Previous progress found
 Do you want to continue from where you left off?
noTafsirLoaded:
No Tafsir has been loaded!
Press the button above to load a Tafsir
qurater_before_sound: A reminder a quarter of an hour before the call to prayer
qurater_after_sound: A reminder a quarter of an hour after the call to prayer
two_takbirs: Activate the call to prayer with two takbirs
somthingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
clickHereToSpeedMocingBetweenPages: Click here to quickly move between pages

alarmWhenCompleteReadAzkar: Alert when Azkar reading is incomplete
showNotificationsForRemindTheReaders: Show notifications to remind for Azkar reading
hadithExplainAndBenefits: Explanation and Benefits of the Hadith:
caveSurahReminder: Dont forget to recite Cave Surah Reminder !
notePrayerSeettings: Note: Changes will be applied after the next notification.
pleaseAllowPermissionFotGetPrayerTimes: Please allow location permissions at least once to get prayer times data
qiblaDescription: Calibrate the compass every time you use it

iWillreadmyWerdthrough: I will read my Werd through
app'sMoshafDescription: Read through the Mushaf of our Islamuna app
ownMoshafDescription: Read your Werd from your personal Mushaf and track your progress through the app

khatmaDescriptionSubtitle: A brief description to encourage participation
calculateKhatmaSubtitle: Calculate your Khatma using one of two methods
expectedDurationSubtext: By specifying the number of days to complete the Khatma
dailyWerdRateSubtext: By the amount you want to read each time
addKhatmaDurationHeader: What is the duration you intend to complete your Khatma?
startKhatmaPositionHeader: From which part of the Quran do you want to start your Khatma?
expectedDuration: Expected duration to complete the Khatma
set_alarm_description: Set if you want us to remind you of your daily reading time

duration_must_be_integer: The duration must be an integer
duration_not_less_than_one: The duration cannot be less than 1
can_complete_after_24_hours: You can complete again after 24 hours from the last completion
